generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures input current to the permanent magnet synchronous motor (I_M) for torque calculation using manufacturer-provided motor constants. Connected to Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor shaft. Provides high-resolution angular position data for backlash reduction and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox to measure output shaft position. Enables comparison with motor encoder readings to infer drive train elasticity and friction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CNCController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller providing access to actual and target position, speed, acceleration data for the first axis. Streams operational parameters via MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CNCControllerDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance CNCControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CNCController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing measured motor input current (I_M) in amperes, converted to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer parameters. Linked to friction analysis models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionModel, DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "High-resolution angular position data (r_M) from motor encoder in degrees, used for velocity calculation and backlash compensation. Time-series with timestamp alignment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLStructuralModel, SimulinkFrictionModel
]

instance GearboxEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output shaft angular position data (r_out) from gearbox encoder in degrees, enabling comparison with motor encoder readings to detect gearbox friction and elasticity."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLStructuralModel, DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance CNCControllerData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operational parameters including actual/target position, speed, and acceleration data for the first axis, streamed via MQTT from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CNCControllerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLBehavioralModel, VisualizationDashboard
]

instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) representing the structural composition of the first axis including permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MBSEEnabler
]

instance SysMLBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram modeling the behavioral relationships between motor torque, gearbox friction, and output velocity for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MBSEEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink model implementing combined white-box (Coulomb-Viscous) and black-box friction identification algorithms for real-time joint friction dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance TorqueVelocityDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model converting motor current (I_M) to torque (τ_M) and encoder readings to angular velocity (ω_M) using manufacturer-provided parameters and dynamics equations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedFrictionlessModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based reference model simulating frictionless behavior of the gearbox and individual components (motor, gearwheel, gearbox) for deviation analysis against measured data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace containing arrays of measured torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) from motor and gearbox encoders, with metadata linking to physical sensors and processing components."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace containing simulated torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) from frictionless models, used for comparison with measured data traces to identify friction-induced deviations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MBSEEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based Model-Based Systems Engineering tool enabling creation and maintenance of structural (BDDs) and behavioral (IBDs) models for the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelingService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment for executing friction models and simulating system behavior with real-time data integration capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, MultiPhysicsAnalysisService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data processing pipeline converting raw sensor data into processed torque/velocity traces and enabling real-time friction parameter identification."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTraceService, ModelValidationService
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Semantic visualization dashboard aggregating and comparing simulated vs. measured data traces to support deviation analysis and physical relationship understanding."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Industry 4.0 Asset Administration Shell providing semantic metadata linking data traces to the physical MABI MAX 100 robot first axis and enabling modular extension to other axes."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataSemanticLinkingService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides structural and behavioral modeling capabilities using SysML BDDs and IBDs for the first axis components and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralModelInsight
]

instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Combines white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models to derive joint friction dynamics in real-time using Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterUpdateAction, FrictionInducedAccuracyInsight
]

instance MultiPhysicsAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously identifies static, kinetic, and viscous friction losses using live data and simulation models for multi-physics-aware analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossInsight
]

instance DataTraceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Processes and stores time-series data traces (measured torque/velocity) with semantic metadata linking to physical sensors and models."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceVisualizationInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Compares simulated friction models against reference data to assess accuracy and dynamically refine parameters based on real-time deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and visualizes simulated vs. measured data traces to support understanding of physical relationships (load-temperature-friction) and deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PhysicalRelationshipInsight
]

instance DataSemanticLinkingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables semantic linking of data traces to the physical MABI MAX 100 robot first axis through Asset Administration Shell metadata."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetContextInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StructuralModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural representation of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their rotational/current flow relationships."
]

instance FrictionParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusts friction model parameters (Coulomb coefficient, viscous damping) based on real-time deviations between simulated and measured data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionInducedAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30% of motion errors) informing model-based control algorithms for improved robot accuracy."
]

instance FrictionLossInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction losses using multi-physics analysis for operational optimization."
]

instance DataTraceVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of time-series data traces (torque, velocity) enabling comparison between simulated and measured values for deviation analysis."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of friction model accuracy through comparison with reference data, supporting iterative refinement of parameters."
]

instance PhysicalRelationshipInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Understanding of physical relationships between load, temperature, and friction dynamics through dashboard visualizations of data traces."
]

instance AssetContextInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic context provided by Asset Administration Shell linking data traces to the physical MABI MAX 100 robot first axis components."
]

instance WearPatternDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of wear patterns or temperature-induced friction changes enabling proactive maintenance scheduling."
]

instance CompensatoryStrategyAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggests compensatory strategies (e.g., torque adjustments) to mitigate friction losses during machining tasks based on real-time friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates maintenance recommendations based on detected wear patterns or friction changes to optimize robot operational lifespan."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning operates at **real-time or near-real-time scales**, synchronized with the robot's operational frequency. Key time-scale specifics include:
  - **Data acquisition rate**: Sensor data (e.g., encoder readings, current) is streamed continuously via MQTT, enabling **millisecond-level updates** for friction analysis.
  - **Simulation/model update rate**: Friction models (e.g., Simulink) are recomputed dynamically during operation to reflect **operational changes** (e.g., wear, temperature, load).
  - **Visualization refresh rate**: Dashboard updates display deviations between simulated and measured data at intervals aligned with operational events (e.g., axis movements). The system supports **sub-second latency** for friction identification."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as follows:
  - **Purpose-driven design**: The digital shadow is tailored to **friction identification** in the first axis, with a clear scope (deviation analysis) and solution (model refinement).
  - **Component interactions**:
    1. **Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensor) and virtual sources (derived data traces, e.g., torque from current).
    2. **Processing**: Simulation components (e.g., Simulink models for motor, gearwheel, gearbox) compute data traces using input data (e.g., I_M) and friction models.
    3. **Models**: Engineering (SysML), data calculation (torque/velocity derivation), and simulation (friction models) models are integrated via the **Processing component**.
  - **Data flow**: One-way from PT (sensors) → DT (data traces → processing → visualization), with no feedback to the PT. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** ensures traceability of data origins and computational methods."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the **as-operated** lifecycle stage of the physical twin, focusing on **real-time monitoring and analysis** during the robot's operational phase. It does not address design (ideal) or manufacturing (as-manufactured) phases but instead targets **continuous improvement** of motion accuracy via friction modeling. The DT is **runtime-oriented**, leveraging live data to adapt models dynamically."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model verification**: Simulink-based friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) are validated against **manufacturer-provided parameters** and **measured data traces** (e.g., torque/velocity deviations).
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: The difference between **simulated (ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out)** and **measured (r_M, r_G, r_out)** data traces is analyzed to assess model accuracy. Uncertainties arise from **sensor noise**, **parameter drift** (e.g., gearbox wear), and **model simplifications** (e.g., ignoring higher-order friction effects).
  - **Traceability**: Each data trace is linked to its **source (sensor/processing component)** and **computational model**, enabling root-cause analysis of deviations. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** ensures end-to-end traceability of data generation.
  - **Validation mechanisms**: Continuous comparison of **simulated friction models** with **live measurements** (e.g., encoder readings) to refine parameters dynamically. The system supports **real-time validation** via dashboard visualizations."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between PT and DT includes:
  - **Network protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time data streaming** of sensor data (e.g., encoder readings, current) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Data format**: Time-series data traces (e.g., I_M, r_M) are transmitted as **floating-point arrays** of torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) with metadata (e.g., timestamp, source).
  - **Architecture**: **Client-server model** where the PT (robot) acts as the data provider, and the DT (digital shadow) consumes and processes the data. The connection is **one-way**, with no feedback to the PT.
  - **Latency**: Designed for **low-latency communication** (<100ms) to support real-time friction identification."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed as:
  - **Software environment**: Runs on a **local or cloud-based server** (not specified) with access to **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for model execution and **MQTT broker** for data ingestion.
  - **Execution platform**: The digital shadow pipeline is implemented as an **executable model** (e.g., Simulink-based) with **real-time data processing** capabilities. The **dashboard** is likely deployed as a **web-based application** (e.g., using Python/Flask or similar) to visualize aggregated data.
  - **Scalability**: The methodology allows for **modular deployment** of digital shadows for multiple axes/robots using **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for semantic interoperability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for **structural and behavioral modeling** of the first axis (e.g., Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams).
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Follows the **Industry 4.0 reference architecture** for semantic description of assets (e.g., MABI robot) and their data traces.
  - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**: Standard protocol for **real-time data streaming** from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: A **domain-specific reference model** (UML-based) for structuring data traces, sources, and processing components in digital shadows."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly address **cybersecurity risks** (e.g., data tampering, unauthorized access) or **safety considerations** (e.g., remote operation hazards). However, the following implicit points can be inferred:
  - **No feedback control**: The digital shadow is **passive**, eliminating risks of **unintended PT manipulation** via the DT.
  - **Data integrity**: MQTT-based data streaming assumes **secure transmission** (not specified) to prevent corruption of sensor data (e.g., encoder readings).
  - **Operational safety**: The system focuses on **monitoring and analysis**, not direct PT control, reducing risks of **human accidents** during machining. The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl** controller likely includes built-in safety mechanisms (e.g., emergency stops), but these are not detailed in the document."
]
